   666→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   667→# LINE 4: PWD & GIT STATUS
   668→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   669→# NOTE: is_git_repo, branch, stash_count, modified, staged, untracked, total_changed,
   670→#       ahead, behind, last_commit_epoch are populated by PARALLEL PREFETCH
   671→
   672→# Calculate age display from prefetched last_commit_epoch
   673→if [ "$is_git_repo" = "true" ] && [ -n "$last_commit_epoch" ]; then
   674→    now_epoch=$(date +%s)
   675→    age_seconds=$((now_epoch - last_commit_epoch))
   676→    age_minutes=$((age_seconds / 60))
   677→    age_hours=$((age_seconds / 3600))
   678→    age_days=$((age_seconds / 86400))
   679→
   680→    if   [ "$age_minutes" -lt 1 ];  then age_display="now";         age_color="$GIT_AGE_FRESH"
   681→    elif [ "$age_hours" -lt 1 ];    then age_display="${age_minutes}m"; age_color="$GIT_AGE_FRESH"
   682→    elif [ "$age_hours" -lt 24 ];   then age_display="${age_hours}h";   age_color="$GIT_AGE_RECENT"
   683→    elif [ "$age_days" -lt 7 ];     then age_display="${age_days}d";    age_color="$GIT_AGE_STALE"
   684→    else age_display="${age_days}d"; age_color="$GIT_AGE_OLD"
   685→    fi
   686→
   687→    # Status indicator
   688→    [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] || [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ] && git_status_icon="*" || git_status_icon="✓"
   689→fi
   690→
   691→# Always output PWD line, with git info if available
   692→case "$MODE" in
   693→    nano)
   694→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   695→        [ "$is_git_repo" = true ] && printf " ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET} " && {
   696→            [ "$git_status_icon" = "✓" ] && printf "${GIT_CLEAN}✓${RESET}" || printf "${GIT_MODIFIED}*${total_changed}${RESET}"
   697→        }
   698→        printf "\n"
   699→        ;;
   700→    micro)
   701→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   702→        if [ "$is_git_repo" = true ]; then
   703→            printf " ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET}"
   704→            [ -n "$age_display" ] && printf " ${age_color}${age_display}${RESET}"
   705→            printf " "
   706→            [ "$git_status_icon" = "✓" ] && printf "${GIT_CLEAN}${git_status_icon}${RESET}" || printf "${GIT_MODIFIED}${git_status_icon}${total_changed}${RESET}"
   707→        fi
   708→        printf "\n"
   709→        ;;
   710→    mini)
   711→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   712→        if [ "$is_git_repo" = true ]; then
   713→            printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET}"
   714→            [ -n "$age_display" ] && printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${age_color}${age_display}${RESET}"
   715→            printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   716→            if [ "$git_status_icon" = "✓" ]; then
   717→                printf "${GIT_CLEAN}${git_status_icon}${RESET}"
   718→            else
   719→                printf "${GIT_MODIFIED}${git_status_icon}${total_changed}${RESET}"
   720→                [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ] && printf " ${GIT_ADDED}+${untracked}${RESET}"
   721→            fi
   722→        fi
   723→        printf "\n"
   724→        ;;
   725→    normal)
   726→        printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}◈${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}PWD:${RESET} ${GIT_DIR}${dir_name}${RESET}"
   727→        if [ "$is_git_repo" = true ]; then
   728→            printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Branch:${RESET} ${GIT_VALUE}${branch}${RESET}"
   729→            [ -n "$age_display" ] && printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Age:${RESET} ${age_color}${age_display}${RESET}"
   730→            [ "$stash_count" -gt 0 ] && printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Stash:${RESET} ${GIT_STASH}${stash_count}${RESET}"
   731→            if [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] || [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ]; then
   732→                printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   733→                [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] && printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}Mod:${RESET} ${GIT_MODIFIED}${total_changed}${RESET}"
   734→                [ "$untracked" -gt 0 ] && { [ "$total_changed" -gt 0 ] && printf " "; printf "${GIT_PRIMARY}New:${RESET} ${GIT_ADDED}${untracked}${RESET}"; }
   735→            else
   736→                printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_CLEAN}✓ clean${RESET}"
   737→            fi
   738→            if [ "$ahead" -gt 0 ] || [ "$behind" -gt 0 ]; then
   739→                printf " ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${GIT_PRIMARY}Sync:${RESET} "
   740→                [ "$ahead" -gt 0 ] && printf "${GIT_CLEAN}↑${ahead}${RESET}"
   741→                [ "$behind" -gt 0 ] && printf "${GIT_STASH}↓${behind}${RESET}"
   742→            fi
   743→        fi
   744→        printf "\n"
   745→        ;;
   746→esac
   747→printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"

   751→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   752→
   753→case "$MODE" in
   754→    nano)

   759→        ;;

   765→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}◇${RESET}${SLATE_300}${research_count}${RESET}\n"
   766→        ;;
   767→    normal)
   768→        printf "${LEARN_PRIMARY}◎${RESET} ${LEARN_SECONDARY}MEMORY:${RESET} "
   769→        printf "${LEARN_WORK}📁${RESET}${SLATE_300}${work_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_WORK}Work${RESET} "
   770→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_SIGNALS}✦${RESET}${SLATE_300}${ratings_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SIGNALS}Ratings${RESET} "
   771→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_SESSIONS}⊕${RESET}${SLATE_300}${sessions_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_SESSIONS}Sessions${RESET} "
   772→        printf "${SLATE_600}│${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}◇${RESET}${SLATE_300}${research_count}${RESET} ${LEARN_RESEARCH}Research${RESET}\n"
   773→        ;;
   774→esac
   775→printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"
   776→
   777→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   778→# LINE 6: LEARNING (with sparklines in normal mode)
   779→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   780→
   781→LEARNING_CACHE="$PAI_DIR/MEMORY/STATE/learning-cache.sh"
   782→LEARNING_CACHE_TTL=30  # seconds
   783→
   784→if [ -f "$RATINGS_FILE" ] && [ -s "$RATINGS_FILE" ]; then
   785→    now=$(date +%s)
   786→
   787→    # Check cache validity (by mtime and ratings file mtime)
   788→    cache_valid=false
   789→    if [ -f "$LEARNING_CACHE" ]; then
   790→        cache_mtime=$(stat -f %m "$LEARNING_CACHE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   791→        ratings_mtime=$(stat -f %m "$RATINGS_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
   792→        cache_age=$((now - cache_mtime))
   793→        # Cache valid if: cache newer than ratings AND cache age < TTL
   794→        if [ "$cache_mtime" -gt "$ratings_mtime" ] && [ "$cache_age" -lt "$LEARNING_CACHE_TTL" ]; then
   795→            cache_valid=true
   796→        fi
   797→    fi
   798→
   799→    if [ "$cache_valid" = true ]; then
   800→        # Use cached values
   801→        source "$LEARNING_CACHE"
   802→    else
   803→        # Compute fresh and cache
   804→        eval "$(jq -rs --argjson now "$now" '
   805→      # Parse ISO timestamp to epoch (handles timezone offsets)
   806→      def to_epoch:
   807→        (capture("(?<sign>[-+])(?<h>[0-9]{2}):(?<m>[0-9]{2})$") // {sign: "+", h: "00", m: "00"}) as $tz |
   808→        gsub("[-+][0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$"; "Z") | gsub("\\.[0-9]+"; "") | fromdateiso8601 |
   809→        . + (if $tz.sign == "-" then 1 else -1 end) * (($tz.h | tonumber) * 3600 + ($tz.m | tonumber) * 60);
   810→
   811→      # Filter valid ratings and add epoch
   812→      [.[] | select(.rating != null) | . + {epoch: (.timestamp | to_epoch)}] |
   813→
   814→      # Time boundaries
   815→      ($now - 900) as $q15_start | ($now - 3600) as $hour_start | ($now - 86400) as $today_start |
   816→      ($now - 604800) as $week_start | ($now - 2592000) as $month_start |
   817→
   818→      # Calculate averages
   819→      (map(select(.epoch >= $q15_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $q15_avg |
   820→      (map(select(.epoch >= $hour_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $hour_avg |
   821→      (map(select(.epoch >= $today_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $today_avg |
   822→      (map(select(.epoch >= $week_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $week_avg |
   823→      (map(select(.epoch >= $month_start) | .rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $month_avg |
   824→      (map(.rating) | if length > 0 then (add / length | . * 10 | floor / 10 | tostring) else "—" end) as $all_avg |
   825→
   826→      # Sparkline: diverging from 5, symmetric heights, color = direction
   827→      def to_bar:
   828→        floor |
   829→        if . >= 10 then "\u001b[38;2;34;197;94m▅\u001b[0m"      # brightest green
   830→        elif . >= 9 then "\u001b[38;2;74;222;128m▅\u001b[0m"    # green
   831→        elif . >= 8 then "\u001b[38;2;134;239;172m▄\u001b[0m"   # light green
   832→        elif . >= 7 then "\u001b[38;2;59;130;246m▃\u001b[0m"    # dark blue
   833→        elif . >= 6 then "\u001b[38;2;96;165;250m▂\u001b[0m"    # blue
   834→        elif . >= 5 then "\u001b[38;2;253;224;71m▁\u001b[0m"    # yellow baseline
   835→        elif . >= 4 then "\u001b[38;2;253;186;116m▂\u001b[0m"   # light orange
   836→        elif . >= 3 then "\u001b[38;2;251;146;60m▃\u001b[0m"    # orange
   837→        elif . >= 2 then "\u001b[38;2;248;113;113m▄\u001b[0m"   # light red
   838→        else "\u001b[38;2;239;68;68m▅\u001b[0m" end;            # red
   839→
   840→      def make_sparkline($period_start):
   841→        . as $all | ($now - $period_start) as $dur | ($dur / 58) as $sz |
   842→        [range(58) | . as $i | ($period_start + ($i * $sz)) as $s | ($s + $sz) as $e |
   843→          [$all[] | select(.epoch >= $s and .epoch < $e) | .rating] |
   844→          if length == 0 then "\u001b[38;2;45;50;60m \u001b[0m" else (add / length) | to_bar end
   845→        ] | join("");
   846→
   847→      (make_sparkline($q15_start)) as $q15_sparkline |
   848→      (make_sparkline($hour_start)) as $hour_sparkline |
   849→      (make_sparkline($today_start)) as $day_sparkline |
   850→      (make_sparkline($week_start)) as $week_sparkline |
   851→      (make_sparkline($month_start)) as $month_sparkline |
   852→
   853→      # Trend calculation helper
   854→      def calc_trend($data):
   855→        if ($data | length) >= 2 then
   856→          (($data | length) / 2 | floor) as $half |
   857→          ($data[-$half:] | add / length) as $recent |
   858→          ($data[:$half] | add / length) as $older |
   859→          ($recent - $older) | if . > 0.5 then "up" elif . < -0.5 then "down" else "stable" end
   860→        else "stable" end;
   861→
   862→      # Friendly summary helper (8 words max)
   863→      def friendly_summary($avg; $trend; $period):
   864→        if $avg == "—" then "No data yet for \($period)"
   865→        elif ($avg | tonumber) >= 8 then
   866→          if $trend == "up" then "Excellent and improving" elif $trend == "down" then "Great but cooling slightly" else "Smooth sailing, all good" end
   867→        elif ($avg | tonumber) >= 6 then
   868→          if $trend == "up" then "Good and getting better" elif $trend == "down" then "Okay but trending down" else "Solid, steady performance" end
   869→        elif ($avg | tonumber) >= 4 then
   870→          if $trend == "up" then "Recovering, headed right direction" elif $trend == "down" then "Needs attention, declining" else "Mixed results, room to improve" end
   871→        else
   872→          if $trend == "up" then "Rough but improving now" elif $trend == "down" then "Struggling, needs focus" else "Challenging period, stay sharp" end
   873→        end;
   874→
   875→      # Hour and day trends
   876→      ([.[] | select(.epoch >= $hour_start) | .rating]) as $hour_data |
   877→      ([.[] | select(.epoch >= $today_start) | .rating]) as $day_data |
   878→      (calc_trend($hour_data)) as $hour_trend |
   879→      (calc_trend($day_data)) as $day_trend |
   880→
   881→      # Generate friendly summaries
   882→      (friendly_summary($hour_avg; $hour_trend; "hour")) as $hour_summary |
   883→      (friendly_summary($today_avg; $day_trend; "day")) as $day_summary |
   884→
   885→      # Overall trend
   886→      length as $total |
   887→      (if $total >= 4 then
   888→        (($total / 2) | floor) as $half |
   889→        (.[- $half:] | map(.rating) | add / length) as $recent |
   890→        (.[:$half] | map(.rating) | add / length) as $older |
   891→        ($recent - $older) | if . > 0.3 then "up" elif . < -0.3 then "down" else "stable" end
   892→      else "stable" end) as $trend |
   893→
   894→      (last | .rating | tostring) as $latest |
   895→      (last | .source // "explicit") as $latest_source |
   896→
   897→      "latest=\($latest | @sh)\nlatest_source=\($latest_source | @sh)\n" +
   898→      "q15_avg=\($q15_avg | @sh)\nhour_avg=\($hour_avg | @sh)\ntoday_avg=\($today_avg | @sh)\n" +
   899→      "week_avg=\($week_avg | @sh)\nmonth_avg=\($month_avg | @sh)\nall_avg=\($all_avg | @sh)\n" +
   900→      "q15_sparkline=\($q15_sparkline | @sh)\nhour_sparkline=\($hour_sparkline | @sh)\nday_sparkline=\($day_sparkline | @sh)\n" +
   901→      "week_sparkline=\($week_sparkline | @sh)\nmonth_sparkline=\($month_sparkline | @sh)\n" +
   902→      "hour_trend=\($hour_trend | @sh)\nday_trend=\($day_trend | @sh)\n" +
   903→      "hour_summary=\($hour_summary | @sh)\nday_summary=\($day_summary | @sh)\n" +
   904→      "trend=\($trend | @sh)\ntotal_count=\($total)"
   905→    ' "$RATINGS_FILE" 2>/dev/null)"
   906→
   907→        # Save to cache for next time
   908→        cat > "$LEARNING_CACHE" << CACHE_EOF
   909→latest='$latest'
   910→latest_source='$latest_source'
   911→q15_avg='$q15_avg'
   912→hour_avg='$hour_avg'
   913→today_avg='$today_avg'
   914→week_avg='$week_avg'
   915→month_avg='$month_avg'
   916→all_avg='$all_avg'
   917→q15_sparkline='$q15_sparkline'
   918→hour_sparkline='$hour_sparkline'
   919→day_sparkline='$day_sparkline'
   920→week_sparkline='$week_sparkline'
   921→month_sparkline='$month_sparkline'
   922→hour_trend='$hour_trend'
   923→day_trend='$day_trend'
   924→hour_summary='$hour_summary'
   925→day_summary='$day_summary'
   926→trend='$trend'
   927→total_count=$total_count
   928→CACHE_EOF
   929→    fi  # end cache computation
   930→
   931→    if [ "$total_count" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
   932→        # Trend icon/color
   933→        case "$trend" in
   934→            up)   trend_icon="↗"; trend_color="$EMERALD" ;;
   935→            down) trend_icon="↘"; trend_color="$ROSE" ;;
   936→            *)    trend_icon="→"; trend_color="$SLATE_400" ;;
   937→        esac
   938→
   939→        # Get colors
   940→        [ "$q15_avg" != "—" ] && pulse_base="$q15_avg" || { [ "$hour_avg" != "—" ] && pulse_base="$hour_avg" || { [ "$today_avg" != "—" ] && pulse_base="$today_avg" || pulse_base="$all_avg"; }; }
   941→        PULSE_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "$pulse_base")
   942→        LATEST_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${latest:-5}")
   943→        Q15_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${q15_avg:-5}")
   944→        HOUR_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${hour_avg:-5}")
   945→        TODAY_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${today_avg:-5}")
   946→        WEEK_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${week_avg:-5}")
   947→        MONTH_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "${month_avg:-5}")
   948→        ALL_COLOR=$(get_rating_color "$all_avg")
   949→
   950→        [ "$latest_source" = "explicit" ] && src_label="EXP" || src_label="IMP"
   951→
   952→        case "$MODE" in
   953→            nano)
   954→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET}\n"
   955→                ;;
   956→            micro)
   957→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1h:${RESET} ${HOUR_COLOR}${hour_avg}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1w:${RESET} ${WEEK_COLOR}${week_avg}${RESET}\n"
   958→                ;;
   959→            mini)
   960→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET} ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   961→                printf "${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET} "
   962→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1h:${RESET} ${HOUR_COLOR}${hour_avg}${RESET} "
   963→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET} "
   964→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1w:${RESET} ${WEEK_COLOR}${week_avg}${RESET}\n"
   965→                ;;
   966→            normal)
   967→                printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET} ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   968→                printf "${LATEST_COLOR}${latest}${RESET}${SLATE_500}${src_label}${RESET} ${SLATE_600}│${RESET} "
   969→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}15m:${RESET} ${Q15_COLOR}${q15_avg}${RESET} "
   970→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}60m:${RESET} ${HOUR_COLOR}${hour_avg}${RESET} "
   971→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1d:${RESET} ${TODAY_COLOR}${today_avg}${RESET} "
   972→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1w:${RESET} ${WEEK_COLOR}${week_avg}${RESET} "
   973→                printf "${SIGNAL_PERIOD}1mo:${RESET} ${MONTH_COLOR}${month_avg}${RESET}\n"
   974→
   975→                # Sparklines (condensed, no blank lines)
   976→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "15m:" "$q15_sparkline"
   977→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "60m:" "$hour_sparkline"
   978→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "1d:" "$day_sparkline"
   979→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}├─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "1w:" "$week_sparkline"
   980→                printf "   ${SLATE_600}└─${RESET} ${SIGNAL_PERIOD}%-5s${RESET} %s\n" "1mo:" "$month_sparkline"
   981→                ;;
   982→        esac
   983→    else
   984→        printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET}\n"
   985→        printf "  ${SLATE_500}No ratings yet${RESET}\n"
   986→    fi
   987→else
   988→    printf "${LEARN_LABEL}✿${RESET} ${LEARN_LABEL}LEARNING:${RESET}\n"
   989→    printf "  ${SLATE_500}No ratings yet${RESET}\n"
   990→fi
   991→
   992→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   993→# LINE 7: QUOTE (normal mode only)
   994→# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   995→
   996→if [ "$MODE" = "normal" ]; then
   997→    printf "${SLATE_600}────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────${RESET}\n"
   998→
   999→    # Refresh quote if stale (>30s)
  1000→    quote_age=$(($(date +%s) - $(stat -f %m "$QUOTE_CACHE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)))
  1001→    if [ "$quote_age" -gt 30 ] || [ ! -f "$QUOTE_CACHE" ]; then
  1002→        if [ -n "${ZENQUOTES_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
  1003→            new_quote=$(curl -s --max-time 1 "https://zenquotes.io/api/random/${ZENQUOTES_API_KEY}" 2>/dev/null | \
  1004→                jq -r '.[0] | select(.q | length < 80) | .q + "|" + .a' 2>/dev/null)
  1005→            [ -n "$new_quote" ] && [ "$new_quote" != "null" ] && echo "$new_quote" > "$QUOTE_CACHE"
  1006→        fi
  1007→    fi
  1008→
  1009→    if [ -f "$QUOTE_CACHE" ]; then
  1010→        IFS='|' read -r quote_text quote_author < "$QUOTE_CACHE"
  1011→        author_suffix="\" —${quote_author}"
  1012→        author_len=${#author_suffix}
  1013→        quote_len=${#quote_text}
  1014→        max_line=72
  1015→
  1016→        # Full display: ✦ "quote text" —Author
  1017→        full_len=$((quote_len + author_len + 4))  # 4 for ✦ "
  1018→
  1019→        if [ "$full_len" -le "$max_line" ]; then
  1020→            # Fits on one line
  1021→            printf "${QUOTE_PRIMARY}✦${RESET} ${SLATE_400}\"${quote_text}\"${RESET} ${QUOTE_AUTHOR}—${quote_author}${RESET}\n"
  1022→        else
  1023→            # Need to wrap - target ~10 words (55-60 chars) on first line
  1024→            # Line 1 gets: "✦ \"" (4) + text
  1025→            line1_text_max=60  # ~10 words worth
  1026→
  1027→            # Only wrap if there's substantial content left for line 2
  1028→            min_line2=12
  1029→
  1030→            # Target: put ~60 chars on line 1
  1031→            target_line1=$line1_text_max
  1032→            [ "$target_line1" -gt "$quote_len" ] && target_line1=$((quote_len - min_line2))
  1033→
  1034→            # Find word boundary near target
  1035→            first_part="${quote_text:0:$target_line1}"
  1036→            remaining="${quote_text:$target_line1}"
  1037→
  1038→            # If we're not at a space, find the last space in first_part
  1039→            if [ -n "$remaining" ] && [ "${remaining:0:1}" != " " ]; then
  1040→                # Find last space position
  1041→                temp="$first_part"
  1042→                last_space_pos=0
  1043→                pos=0
  1044→                while [ $pos -lt ${#temp} ]; do
  1045→                    [ "${temp:$pos:1}" = " " ] && last_space_pos=$pos
  1046→                    pos=$((pos + 1))
  1047→                done
  1048→                if [ $last_space_pos -gt 10 ]; then
  1049→                    first_part="${quote_text:0:$last_space_pos}"
  1050→                fi
  1051→            fi
  1052→
  1053→            second_part="${quote_text:${#first_part}}"
  1054→            second_part="${second_part# }"  # trim leading space
  1055→
  1056→            # Only wrap if second part is substantial (more than just a few words)
  1057→            if [ ${#second_part} -lt 10 ]; then
  1058→                # Too little for line 2, just print on one line (may overflow slightly)
  1059→                printf "${QUOTE_PRIMARY}✦${RESET} ${SLATE_400}\"${quote_text}\"${RESET} ${QUOTE_AUTHOR}—${quote_author}${RESET}\n"
  1060→            else
  1061→                printf "${QUOTE_PRIMARY}✦${RESET} ${SLATE_400}\"${first_part}${RESET}\n"
  1062→                printf "  ${SLATE_400}${second_part}\"${RESET} ${QUOTE_AUTHOR}—${quote_author}${RESET}\n"
  1063→            fi
  1064→        fi
  1065→    fi
  1066→fi

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Shared Learning Utilities
     3→ *
     4→ * Categorization logic for learnings across the hook system.
     5→ * Used by: ExplicitRatingCapture, ImplicitSentimentCapture, ResponseCapture
     6→ */
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * Categorize learning as SYSTEM (tooling/infrastructure) or ALGORITHM (task execution)
    10→ *
    11→ * SYSTEM = hook failures, tooling issues, infrastructure problems, system errors
    12→ * ALGORITHM = task execution issues, approach errors, method improvements
    13→ *
    14→ * Check ALGORITHM first because user feedback about task execution is more valuable.
    15→ * Default to ALGORITHM since most learnings are about task quality, not infrastructure.
    16→ *
    17→ * @param content - The main content to analyze
    18→ * @param comment - Optional user comment to include in analysis
    19→ */
    20→export function getLearningCategory(content: string, comment?: string): 'SYSTEM' | 'ALGORITHM' {
    21→  const text = `${content} ${comment || ''}`.toLowerCase();
    22→
    23→  // ALGORITHM indicators - task execution/approach issues (check first)
    24→  const algorithmIndicators = [
    25→    /over.?engineer/,
    26→    /wrong approach/,
    27→    /should have asked/,
    28→    /didn't follow/,
    29→    /missed the point/,
    30→    /too complex/,
    31→    /didn't understand/,
    32→    /wrong direction/,
    33→    /not what i wanted/,
    34→    /approach|method|strategy|reasoning/
    35→  ];
    36→
    37→  // SYSTEM indicators - tooling/infrastructure issues
    38→  const systemIndicators = [
    39→    /hook|crash|broken/,
    40→    /tool|config|deploy|path/,
    41→    /import|module|file.*not.*found/,
    42→    /typescript|javascript|npm|bun/
    43→  ];
    44→
    45→  // Check ALGORITHM first (user feedback about approach is valuable)
    46→  for (const pattern of algorithmIndicators) {
    47→    if (pattern.test(text)) return 'ALGORITHM';
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  for (const pattern of systemIndicators) {
    51→    if (pattern.test(text)) return 'SYSTEM';
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  // Default: learnings reflect task quality → ALGORITHM
    55→  return 'ALGORITHM';
    56→}
    57→
    58→/**
    59→ * Determine if a response represents a learning moment
    60→ */
    61→export function isLearningCapture(text: string, summary?: string, analysis?: string): boolean {
    62→  const learningIndicators = [
    63→    /problem|issue|bug|error|failed|broken/i,
    64→    /fixed|solved|resolved|discovered|realized|learned/i,
    65→    /troubleshoot|debug|investigate|root cause/i,
    66→    /lesson|takeaway|now we know|next time/i,
    67→  ];
    68→
    69→  const checkText = `${summary || ''} ${analysis || ''} ${text}`;
    70→
    71→  let indicatorCount = 0;
    72→  for (const pattern of learningIndicators) {
    73→    if (pattern.test(checkText)) {
    74→      indicatorCount++;
    75→    }
    76→  }
    77→
    78→  // If 2+ learning indicators, consider it a learning
    79→  return indicatorCount >= 2;
    80→}
    81→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

input.content:
# VoiceServer Windows Startup Script
# PAI v2.5 - Cross-Platform Voice Notification Server

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

$VoiceDir = "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\VoiceServer"
$PidFile = "$VoiceDir\voice.pid"
$LogFile = "$VoiceDir\voice.log"
$Port = 8888

Write-Host "Starting VoiceServer..." -ForegroundColor Green

# Check if already running
if (Test-Path $PidFile) {
    try {
        $oldPid = Get-Content $PidFile -ErrorAction Stop
        $process = Get-Process -Id $oldPid -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        if ($process) {
            Write-Host "VoiceServer is already running (PID: $oldPid)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
            exit 1
        } else {
            Remove-Item $PidFile -Force
        }
    } catch {
        Remove-Item $PidFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }
}

# Check if Python is installed
$pythonCmd = Get-Command python -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if (-not $pythonCmd) {
    $pythonCmd = Get-Command python3 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
}

if (-not $pythonCmd) {
    Write-Host "Error: Python not found. Please install Python 3.8+" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

Write-Host "Using Python: $($pythonCmd.Source)" -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Check if uvicorn is installed
$uvicornCmd = Get-Command uvicorn -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if (-not $uvicornCmd) {
    Write-Host "Installing uvicorn..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    & $pythonCmd.Source -m pip install uvicorn fastapi
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
        Write-Host "Error: Failed to install uvicorn" -ForegroundColor Red
        exit 1
    }
}

# Check if server.py exists
if (-not (Test-Path "$VoiceDir\server.py")) {
    Write-Host "Error: server.py not found in $VoiceDir" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

# Start the server using uvicorn
Write-Host "Starting uvicorn server on port $Port..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

$env:PYTHONUNBUFFERED = "1"

# Start process in background
$processStartInfo = New-Object System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo
$processStartInfo.FileName = $pythonCmd.Source
$processStartInfo.Arguments = "-m uvicorn server:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port $Port --log-level info"
$processStartInfo.WorkingDirectory = $VoiceDir
$processStartInfo.UseShellExecute = $false
$processStartInfo.RedirectStandardOutput = $true
$processStartInfo.RedirectStandardError = $true
$processStartInfo.CreateNoWindow = $true

$process = New-Object System.Diagnostics.Process
$process.StartInfo = $processStartInfo
$process.Start() | Out-Null

# Save PID
$process.Id | Out-File -FilePath $PidFile -Encoding utf8

# Wait a moment for startup
Start-Sleep -Seconds 2

# Check if still running
if ($process.HasExited) {
    Write-Host "Error: VoiceServer failed to start" -ForegroundColor Red
    Remove-Item $PidFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

    # Try to read error output
    $errorOutput = $process.StandardError.ReadToEnd()
    if ($errorOutput) {
        Write-Host "Error output: $errorOutput" -ForegroundColor Red
    }

    exit 1
}

Write-Host "VoiceServer started successfully (PID: $($process.Id))" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Logs: $LogFile" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Health check: http://127.0.0.1:$Port/health" -ForegroundColor Cyan

input.content:
# Install VoiceServer as Windows Service using NSSM
# PAI v2.5 - Windows Service Installation

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

$ServiceName = "PAIVoiceServer"
$VoiceDir = "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\VoiceServer"
$StartupScript = "$VoiceDir\start.ps1"
$StopScript = "$VoiceDir\stop.ps1"

Write-Host "PAI VoiceServer - Windows Service Installation" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Check if running as Administrator
$isAdmin = ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal] [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)

if (-not $isAdmin) {
    Write-Host "Error: This script requires Administrator privileges" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "Please right-click and select 'Run as Administrator'" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    exit 1
}

# Check if VoiceServer scripts exist
if (-not (Test-Path $StartupScript)) {
    Write-Host "Error: Start script not found: $StartupScript" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

if (-not (Test-Path $StopScript)) {
    Write-Host "Error: Stop script not found: $StopScript" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

# Check if NSSM is available
$nssm = Get-Command nssm -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

if (-not $nssm) {
    Write-Host "NSSM not found. Installing..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

    # Try to install NSSM via winget
    $winget = Get-Command winget -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if ($winget) {
        Write-Host "Installing NSSM via winget..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
        winget install nssm --accept-package-agreements --accept-source-agreements
        $env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
        $nssm = Get-Command nssm -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }
}

if (-not $nssm) {
    Write-Host "Error: NSSM not found. Please install manually:" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "  1. Download from: https://nssm.cc/download" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host "  2. Extract and add to PATH" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "Or use winget: winget install nssm" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    exit 1
}

Write-Host "Using NSSM: $($nssm.Source)" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ""

# Check if service already exists
$existingService = Get-Service -Name $ServiceName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($existingService) {
    Write-Host "Service already exists: $ServiceName" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $response = Read-Host "Remove and reinstall? (y/N)"
    if ($response -eq 'y' -or $response -eq 'Y') {
        Write-Host "Removing existing service..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
        & nssm remove $ServiceName confirm
        Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
    } else {
        Write-Host "Installation cancelled" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        exit 0
    }
}

# Install service
Write-Host "Installing service..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

& nssm install $ServiceName powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File $StartupScript
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
    Write-Host "Error: Failed to install service" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

# Configure service
Write-Host "Configuring service..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

& nssm set $ServiceName AppDirectory $VoiceDir
& nssm set $ServiceName DisplayName "PAI Voice Notification Server"
& nssm set $ServiceName Description "Voice notification server for PAI v2.5 - Local TTS with emotional inference"
& nssm set $ServiceName Start SERVICE_AUTO_START

# Configure service recovery
& nssm set $ServiceName AppRestartDelay 5000
& nssm set $ServiceName AppThrottle 1500
& nssm set $ServiceName AppExit Default Restart
& nssm set $ServiceName AppRestartDelay 5000

# Set stdout/stderr logging
$logDir = "$VoiceDir\logs"
if (-not (Test-Path $logDir)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $logDir -Force | Out-Null
}

& nssm set $ServiceName StdoutLog "$logDir\service-out.log"
& nssm set $ServiceName StderrLog "$logDir\service-err.log"

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Service installed successfully!" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Service Management Commands:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Start service:   Start-Service $ServiceName" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Stop service:    Stop-Service $ServiceName" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Check status:    Get-Service $ServiceName" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  View logs:       Get-Content $logDir\service-out.log" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Remove service:  nssm remove $ServiceName confirm" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Starting service now..." -ForegroundColor Cyan

try {
    Start-Service $ServiceName
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 2

    $service = Get-Service $ServiceName
    if ($service.Status -eq 'Running') {
        Write-Host "Service started successfully!" -ForegroundColor Green
    } else {
        Write-Host "Warning: Service status is $($service.Status)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        Write-Host "Check logs for errors" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    }
} catch {
    Write-Host "Error: Failed to start service" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "Error: $_" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "You can start the service manually:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host "  Start-Service $ServiceName" -ForegroundColor White
}

1→"""Audio playback for macOS using afplay."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→import subprocess
     5→from pathlib import Path
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→import logging
     8→
     9→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    10→
    11→
    12→async def play_audio(
    13→    audio_path: Path | str,
    14→    volume: float = 0.8,
    15→    delete_after: bool = True
    16→) -> bool:
    17→    """
    18→    Play audio file using macOS afplay.
    19→
    20→    Args:
    21→        audio_path: Path to the audio file
    22→        volume: Playback volume (0.0 to 1.0)
    23→        delete_after: Whether to delete the file after playing
    24→
    25→    Returns:
    26→        True if playback succeeded, False otherwise
    27→    """
    28→    audio_path = Path(audio_path)
    29→
    30→    if not audio_path.exists():
    31→        logger.error(f"Audio file not found: {audio_path}")
    32→        return False
    33→
    34→    try:
    35→        # afplay volume is 0-255, but we use 0-1.0 scale
    36→        afplay_volume = max(0, min(255, int(volume * 255)))
    37→
    38→        # Run afplay asynchronously
    39→        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
    40→            "afplay",
    41→            "-v", str(volume),  # afplay uses 0.0-1.0 scale directly
    42→            str(audio_path),
    43→            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
    44→            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE
    45→        )
    46→
    47→        _, stderr = await process.communicate()
    48→
    49→        if process.returncode != 0:
    50→            logger.error(f"afplay failed: {stderr.decode()}")
    51→            return False
    52→
    53→        logger.info(f"Played audio: {audio_path}")
    54→        return True
    55→
    56→    except FileNotFoundError:
    57→        logger.error("afplay not found - is this macOS?")
    58→        return False
    59→    except Exception as e:
    60→        logger.error(f"Audio playback error: {e}")
    61→        return False
    62→    finally:
    63→        # Clean up temp file if requested
    64→        if delete_after and audio_path.exists():
    65→            try:
    66→                audio_path.unlink()
    67→                logger.debug(f"Deleted temp audio: {audio_path}")
    68→            except Exception as e:
    69→                logger.warning(f"Failed to delete temp audio: {e}")
    70→
    71→
    72→def play_audio_sync(
    73→    audio_path: Path | str,
    74→    volume: float = 0.8,
    75→    delete_after: bool = True
    76→) -> bool:
    77→    """Synchronous version of play_audio."""
    78→    audio_path = Path(audio_path)
    79→
    80→    if not audio_path.exists():
    81→        logger.error(f"Audio file not found: {audio_path}")
    82→        return False
    83→
    84→    try:
    85→        result = subprocess.run(
    86→            ["afplay", "-v", str(volume), str(audio_path)],
    87→            capture_output=True,
    88→            text=True
    89→        )
    90→
    91→        if result.returncode != 0:
    92→            logger.error(f"afplay failed: {result.stderr}")
    93→            return False
    94→
    95→        return True
    96→
    97→    except FileNotFoundError:
    98→        logger.error("afplay not found - is this macOS?")
    99→        return False
   100→    except Exception as e:
   101→        logger.error(f"Audio playback error: {e}")
   102→        return False
   103→    finally:
   104→        if delete_after and audio_path.exists():
   105→            try:
   106→                audio_path.unlink()
   107→            except Exception:
   108→                pass
   109→

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bun
     2→/**
     3→ * WorkCompletionLearning.hook.ts - Extract Learnings from Completed Work (SessionEnd)
     4→ *
     5→ * PURPOSE:
     6→ * Bridges the WORK/ system to the LEARNING/ system. When a session ends with
     7→ * significant work completed, this hook captures the work metadata (files changed,
     8→ * tools used, ideal state criteria) and creates a learning file for future reference.
     9→ * This ensures insights compound over time rather than being lost.
    10→ *
    11→ * TRIGGER: SessionEnd
    12→ *
    13→ * INPUT:
    14→ * - stdin: Hook input JSON (session_id, transcript_path)
    15→ * - Files: MEMORY/STATE/current-work.json, MEMORY/WORK/<dir>/META.yaml
    16→ *
    17→ * OUTPUT:
    18→ * - stdout: None
    19→ * - stderr: Status messages
    20→ * - exit(0): Always (non-blocking)
    21→ *
    22→ * SIDE EFFECTS:
    23→ * - Creates: MEMORY/LEARNING/<category>/<YYYY-MM>/<datetime>_work_<slug>.md
    24→ * - Reads: Current work state and work directory metadata
    25→ *
    26→ * INTER-HOOK RELATIONSHIPS:
    27→ * - DEPENDS ON: AutoWorkCreation (expects WORK/ structure)
    28→ * - COORDINATES WITH: SessionSummary (both run at SessionEnd)
    29→ * - MUST RUN BEFORE: SessionSummary (captures before state is cleared)
    30→ * - MUST RUN AFTER: Stop handlers (captures completed work)
    31→ *
    32→ * SIGNIFICANT WORK CRITERIA:
    33→ * A learning is only captured if:
    34→ * - Files were changed, OR
    35→ * - Multiple items exist in work directory, OR
    36→ * - Work was manually created (source: MANUAL)
    37→ *
    38→ * LEARNING CATEGORIES:
    39→ * - ALGORITHM: Insights about process/approach improvement
    40→ * - SYSTEM: Technical system improvements

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bun
     2→/**
     3→ * ExplicitRatingCapture.hook.ts - Capture Explicit User Ratings (UserPromptSubmit)
     4→ *
     5→ * PURPOSE:
     6→ * Detects when the user explicitly rates a response with a number 1-10.
     7→ * This is the primary feedback mechanism for the rating/sentiment system.
     8→ * Low ratings trigger automatic learning capture for improvement analysis.
     9→ *
    10→ * TRIGGER: UserPromptSubmit
    11→ *
    12→ * INPUT:
    13→ * - prompt: User's message text
    14→ * - session_id: Current session identifier
    15→ * - transcript_path: Path to conversation transcript
    16→ *
    17→ * OUTPUT:
    18→ * - stdout: None (no context injection)
    19→ * - exit(0): Normal completion
    20→ *
    21→ * SIDE EFFECTS:
    22→ * - Writes to: MEMORY/LEARNING/SIGNALS/ratings.jsonl
    23→ * - Writes to: MEMORY/LEARNING/<category>/<YYYY-MM>/*.md (for low ratings)
    24→ * - Triggers: TrendingAnalysis.ts update (fire-and-forget)
    25→ *
    26→ * INTER-HOOK RELATIONSHIPS:
    27→ * - DEPENDS ON: None
    28→ * - COORDINATES WITH: ImplicitSentimentCapture (shares ratings.jsonl)
    29→ * - MUST RUN BEFORE: ImplicitSentimentCapture (explicit takes priority)
    30→ * - MUST RUN AFTER: None
    31→ *
    32→ * COORDINATION PROTOCOL:
    33→ * Both ExplicitRatingCapture and ImplicitSentimentCapture write to ratings.jsonl.
    34→ * ImplicitSentimentCapture checks isExplicitRating() first and defers if true.
    35→ * This hook runs FIRST to capture explicit "8 - great work" style ratings.
    36→ *
    37→ * ERROR HANDLING:
    38→ * - Invalid input: Exits gracefully
    39→ * - Write failures: Logged to stderr, exits gracefully
    40→ *
    41→ * PERFORMANCE:
    42→ * - Non-blocking: Yes
    43→ * - Typical execution: <50ms
    44→ * - No external API calls
    45→ *
    46→ * RATING PATTERNS:
    47→ * - "7" → rating 7, no comment
    48→ * - "8 - good work" → rating 8, comment "good work"
    49→ * - "6: needs improvement" → rating 6, comment "needs improvement"
    50→ * - "9 excellent" → rating 9, comment "excellent"
    51→ * - "10!" → rating 10, no comment
    52→ *
    53→ * NON-RATING PATTERNS (ignored):
    54→ * - "3 items" → Not a rating (followed by unit)
    55→ * - "5 things to fix" → Not a rating (sentence continuation)
    56→ */
    57→
    58→import { appendFileSync, mkdirSync, existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'fs';
    59→import { join } from 'path';
    60→import { getLearningCategory } from './lib/learning-utils';
    61→import { getPrincipalName } from './lib/identity';
    62→import { getISOTimestamp, getPSTComponents } from './lib/time';
    63→import { captureFailure } from '../skills/PAI/Tools/FailureCapture';
    64→import { getPaiDir } from './lib/paths';
    65→
    66→interface HookInput {
    67→  session_id: string;
    68→  prompt: string;
    69→  transcript_path: string;
    70→  hook_event_name: string;
    71→}
    72→
    73→interface RatingEntry {
    74→  timestamp: string;
    75→  rating: number;
    76→  comment?: string;
    77→  session_id: string;
    78→}
    79→
    80→/**
    81→ * Read stdin with timeout
    82→ */
    83→async function readStdinWithTimeout(timeout: number = 5000): Promise<string> {
    84→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    85→    let data = '';
    86→    const timer = setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Timeout')), timeout);
    87→    process.stdin.on('data', (chunk) => { data += chunk.toString(); });
    88→    process.stdin.on('end', () => { clearTimeout(timer); resolve(data); });
    89→    process.stdin.on('error', (err) => { clearTimeout(timer); reject(err); });
    90→  });
    91→}
    92→
    93→/**
    94→ * CRITICAL: Precise rating detection
    95→ *
    96→ * Must match:
    97→ * - "7" (single number)
    98→ * - "8 - good work" (number + dash + comment)
    99→ * - "6: needs work" (number + colon + comment)
   100→ * - "9 excellent" (number + space + comment)

input.content:
"""Cross-platform audio playback for VoiceServer."""

import asyncio
import subprocess
import platform
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Detect platform
IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
IS_MAC = platform.system() == "Darwin"
IS_LINUX = platform.system() == "Linux"


async def play_audio(
    audio_path: Path | str,
    volume: float = 0.8,
    delete_after: bool = True
) -> bool:
    """
    Play audio file using platform-specific audio player.

    Supports:
    - Windows: PowerShell SoundPlayer
    - macOS: afplay
    - Linux: paplay (PulseAudio) or aplay (ALSA)

    Args:
        audio_path: Path to the audio file
        volume: Playback volume (0.0 to 1.0)
        delete_after: Whether to delete the file after playing

    Returns:
        True if playback succeeded, False otherwise
    """
    audio_path = Path(audio_path)

    if not audio_path.exists():
        logger.error(f"Audio file not found: {audio_path}")
        return False

    try:
        if IS_WINDOWS:
            return await _play_windows(audio_path, volume)
        elif IS_MAC:
            return await _play_macos(audio_path, volume)
        elif IS_LINUX:
            return await _play_linux(audio_path, volume)
        else:
            logger.error(f"Unsupported platform: {platform.system()}")
            return False
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Audio playback error: {e}")
        return False
    finally:
        # Clean up temp file if requested
        if delete_after and audio_path.exists():
            try:
                audio_path.unlink()
                logger.debug(f"Deleted temp audio: {audio_path}")
            except Exception as e:
                logger.warning(f"Failed to delete temp audio: {e}")


async def _play_windows(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
    """Play audio on Windows using PowerShell SoundPlayer."""
    try:
        # Windows: Use PowerShell with Media.SoundPlayer
        # Note: volume control is limited with SoundPlayer
        cmd = [
            "powershell",
            "-Command",
            f"(New-Object Media.SoundPlayer '{audio_path}').PlaySync()"
        ]

        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
            *cmd,
            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE
        )

        _, stderr = await process.communicate()

        if process.returncode != 0:
            logger.error(f"Windows audio playback failed: {stderr.decode()}")
            return False

        logger.info(f"Played audio on Windows: {audio_path}")
        return True

    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Windows audio playback error: {e}")
        return False


async def _play_macos(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
    """Play audio on macOS using afplay."""
    try:
        # afplay uses 0.0-1.0 volume scale
        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
            "afplay",
            "-v", str(min(1.0, max(0.0, volume))),
            str(audio_path),
            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE
        )

        _, stderr = await process.communicate()

        if process.returncode != 0:
            logger.error(f"afplay failed: {stderr.decode()}")
            return False

        logger.info(f"Played audio on macOS: {audio_path}")
        return True

    except FileNotFoundError:
        logger.error("afplay not found - is this macOS?")
        return False
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"macOS audio playback error: {e}")
        return False


async def _play_linux(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
    """Play audio on Linux using paplay or aplay."""
    # Try paplay (PulseAudio) first, then aplay (ALSA)
    for player, args in [
        ("paplay", ["--volume", str(int(volume * 65536))]),  # paplay uses 0-65536
        ("aplay", [])  # aplay doesn't support volume control
    ]:
        try:
            cmd = [player, str(audio_path)] + args
            process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
                *cmd,
                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE
            )

            _, stderr = await process.communicate()

            if process.returncode == 0:
                logger.info(f"Played audio on Linux using {player}: {audio_path}")
                return True
            else:
                logger.debug(f"{player} failed: {stderr.decode()}, trying next...")

        except FileNotFoundError:
            logger.debug(f"{player} not found, trying next...")
            continue
        except Exception as e:
            logger.debug(f"{player} error: {e}, trying next...")
            continue

    logger.error("No working audio player found on Linux (tried paplay, aplay)")
    return False


def play_audio_sync(
    audio_path: Path | str,
    volume: float = 0.8,
    delete_after: bool = True
) -> bool:
    """Synchronous version of play_audio."""
    audio_path = Path(audio_path)

    if not audio_path.exists():
        logger.error(f"Audio file not found: {audio_path}")
        return False

    try:
        if IS_WINDOWS:
            return _play_windows_sync(audio_path, volume)
        elif IS_MAC:
            return _play_macos_sync(audio_path, volume)
        elif IS_LINUX:
            return _play_linux_sync(audio_path, volume)
        else:
            logger.error(f"Unsupported platform: {platform.system()}")
            return False
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Audio playback error: {e}")
        return False
    finally:
        if delete_after and audio_path.exists():
            try:
                audio_path.unlink()
            except Exception:
                pass


def _play_windows_sync(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
    """Synchronous Windows audio playback."""
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["powershell", "-Command",
             f"(New-Object Media.SoundPlayer '{audio_path}').PlaySync()"],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )

        if result.returncode != 0:
            logger.error(f"Windows audio playback failed: {result.stderr}")
            return False

        return True
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Windows audio playback error: {e}")
        return False


def _play_macos_sync(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
    """Synchronous macOS audio playback."""
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["afplay", "-v", str(volume), str(audio_path)],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True
        )

        if result.returncode != 0:
            logger.error(f"afplay failed: {result.stderr}")
            return False

        return True
    except FileNotFoundError:
        logger.error("afplay not found - is this macOS?")
        return False
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"macOS audio playback error: {e}")
        return False


def _play_linux_sync(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
    """Synchronous Linux audio playback."""
    for player, args in [
        ("paplay", ["--volume", str(int(volume * 65536))]),
        ("aplay", [])
    ]:
        try:
            cmd = [player, str(audio_path)] + args
            result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)

            if result.returncode == 0:
                return True
        except FileNotFoundError:
            continue
        except Exception:
            continue

    logger.error("No working audio player found on Linux")
    return False

    63→                audio_path.unlink()
    64→                logger.debug(f"Deleted temp audio: {audio_path}")
    65→            except Exception as e:
    66→                logger.warning(f"Failed to delete temp audio: {e}")
    67→
    68→
    69→async def _play_windows(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
    70→    """Play audio on Windows using PowerShell SoundPlayer."""
    71→    try:
    72→        # Windows: Use PowerShell with Media.SoundPlayer
    73→        # Note: volume control is limited with SoundPlayer
    74→        cmd = [
    75→            "powershell",
    76→            "-Command",
    77→            f"(New-Object Media.SoundPlayer '{audio_path}').PlaySync()"
    78→        ]
    79→
    80→        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
    81→            *cmd,
    82→            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
    83→            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE
    84→        )
    85→
    86→        _, stderr = await process.communicate()
    87→
    88→        if process.returncode != 0:
    89→            logger.error(f"Windows audio playback failed: {stderr.decode()}")
    90→            return False
    91→
    92→        logger.info(f"Played audio on Windows: {audio_path}")
    93→        return True
    94→
    95→    except Exception as e:
    96→        logger.error(f"Windows audio playback error: {e}")
    97→        return False
    98→
    99→
   100→async def _play_macos(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
   101→    """Play audio on macOS using afplay."""
   102→    try:
   103→        # afplay uses 0.0-1.0 volume scale
   104→        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
   105→            "afplay",
   106→            "-v", str(min(1.0, max(0.0, volume))),
   107→            str(audio_path),
   108→            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
   109→            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE
   110→        )
   111→
   112→        _, stderr = await process.communicate()
   113→
   114→        if process.returncode != 0:
   115→            logger.error(f"afplay failed: {stderr.decode()}")
   116→            return False
   117→
   118→        logger.info(f"Played audio on macOS: {audio_path}")
   119→        return True
   120→
   121→    except FileNotFoundError:
   122→        logger.error("afplay not found - is this macOS?")
   123→        return False
   124→    except Exception as e:
   125→        logger.error(f"macOS audio playback error: {e}")
   126→        return False
   127→
   128→
   129→async def _play_linux(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
   130→    """Play audio on Linux using paplay or aplay."""
   131→    # Try paplay (PulseAudio) first, then aplay (ALSA)
   132→    for player, args in [
   133→        ("paplay", ["--volume", str(int(volume * 65536))]),  # paplay uses 0-65536
   134→        ("aplay", [])  # aplay doesn't support volume control
   135→    ]:
   136→        try:
   137→            cmd = [player, str(audio_path)] + args
   138→            process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
   139→                *cmd,
   140→                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
   141→                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE
   142→            )
   143→
   144→            _, stderr = await process.communicate()
   145→
   146→            if process.returncode == 0:
   147→                logger.info(f"Played audio on Linux using {player}: {audio_path}")
   148→                return True
   149→            else:
   150→                logger.debug(f"{player} failed: {stderr.decode()}, trying next...")
   151→
   152→        except FileNotFoundError:
   153→            logger.debug(f"{player} not found, trying next...")
   154→            continue
   155→        except Exception as e:
   156→            logger.debug(f"{player} error: {e}, trying next...")
   157→            continue
   158→
   159→    logger.error("No working audio player found on Linux (tried paplay, aplay)")
   160→    return False
   161→
   162→
   163→def play_audio_sync(
   164→    audio_path: Path | str,
   165→    volume: float = 0.8,
   166→    delete_after: bool = True
   167→) -> bool:
   168→    """Synchronous version of play_audio."""
   169→    audio_path = Path(audio_path)
   170→
   171→    if not audio_path.exists():
   172→        logger.error(f"Audio file not found: {audio_path}")
   173→        return False
   174→
   175→    try:
   176→        if IS_WINDOWS:
   177→            return _play_windows_sync(audio_path, volume)
   178→        elif IS_MAC:
   179→            return _play_macos_sync(audio_path, volume)
   180→        elif IS_LINUX:
   181→            return _play_linux_sync(audio_path, volume)
   182→        else:
   183→            logger.error(f"Unsupported platform: {platform.system()}")
   184→            return False
   185→    except Exception as e:
   186→        logger.error(f"Audio playback error: {e}")
   187→        return False
   188→    finally:
   189→        if delete_after and audio_path.exists():
   190→            try:
   191→                audio_path.unlink()
   192→            except Exception:
   193→                pass
   194→
   195→
   196→def _play_windows_sync(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
   197→    """Synchronous Windows audio playback."""
   198→    try:
   199→        result = subprocess.run(
   200→            ["powershell", "-Command",
   201→             f"(New-Object Media.SoundPlayer '{audio_path}').PlaySync()"],
   202→            capture_output=True,
   203→            text=True
   204→        )
   205→
   206→        if result.returncode != 0:
   207→            logger.error(f"Windows audio playback failed: {result.stderr}")
   208→            return False
   209→
   210→        return True
   211→    except Exception as e:
   212→        logger.error(f"Windows audio playback error: {e}")
   213→        return False
   214→
   215→
   216→def _play_macos_sync(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
   217→    """Synchronous macOS audio playback."""
   218→    try:
   219→        result = subprocess.run(
   220→            ["afplay", "-v", str(volume), str(audio_path)],
   221→            capture_output=True,
   222→            text=True
   223→        )
   224→
   225→        if result.returncode != 0:
   226→            logger.error(f"afplay failed: {result.stderr}")
   227→            return False
   228→
   229→        return True
   230→    except FileNotFoundError:
   231→        logger.error("afplay not found - is this macOS?")
   232→        return False
   233→    except Exception as e:
   234→        logger.error(f"macOS audio playback error: {e}")
   235→        return False
   236→
   237→
   238→def _play_linux_sync(audio_path: Path, volume: float) -> bool:
   239→    """Synchronous Linux audio playback."""
   240→    for player, args in [
   241→        ("paplay", ["--volume", str(int(volume * 65536))]),
   242→        ("aplay", [])
   243→    ]:
   244→        try:
   245→            cmd = [player, str(audio_path)] + args
   246→            result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
   247→
   248→            if result.returncode == 0:
   249→                return True
   250→        except FileNotFoundError:
   251→            continue
   252→        except Exception:
   253→            continue
   254→
   255→    logger.error("No working audio player found on Linux")
   256→    return False
   257→

input.content:
# Observability Dashboard - Windows Startup Script
# PAI v2.5 - Cross-Platform System Monitoring

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

$ObsDir = "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\Observability"
$PidFile = "$ObsDir\dashboard.pid"
$Port = 8889

Write-Host "Starting PAI Observability Dashboard..." -ForegroundColor Green

# Create Observability directory if it doesn't exist
if (-not (Test-Path $ObsDir)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $ObsDir -Force | Out-Null
}

# Check if already running
if (Test-Path $PidFile) {
    try {
        $oldPid = Get-Content $PidFile -ErrorAction Stop
        $process = Get-Process -Id $oldPid -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        if ($process) {
            Write-Host "Dashboard is already running (PID: $oldPid)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
            Write-Host "Access at: http://localhost:$Port" -ForegroundColor Cyan
            exit 1
        } else {
            Remove-Item $PidFile -Force
        }
    } catch {
        Remove-Item $PidFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }
}

# Try to find dashboard implementation
$dashboardScript = $null
$command = $null

# Check for Python dashboard
$pyDashboard = "$ObsDir\dashboard.py"
if (Test-Path $pyDashboard) {
    $dashboardScript = $pyDashboard
    $command = Get-Command python -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if (-not $command) {
        $command = Get-Command python3 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }
}

# Check for Node.js dashboard
if (-not $dashboardScript) {
    $jsDashboard = "$ObsDir\dashboard.js"
    if (Test-Path $jsDashboard) {
        $dashboardScript = $jsDashboard
        $command = Get-Command node -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }
}

if (-not $dashboardScript -or -not $command) {
    Write-Host "Warning: No dashboard implementation found" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host "Create one of the following:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host "  - $ObsDir\dashboard.py (Python)" -ForegroundColor White
    Write-Host "  - $ObsDir\dashboard.js (Node.js)" -ForegroundColor White
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "For now, starting a simple HTTP server for manual monitoring..." -ForegroundColor Yellow

    # Start simple Python HTTP server
    $command = Get-Command python -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if (-not $command) {
        $command = Get-Command python3 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    }

    if ($command) {
        $env:PORT = $Port
        $processStartInfo = New-Object System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo
        $processStartInfo.FileName = $command.Source
        $processStartInfo.Arguments = "-m http.server $Port"
        $processStartInfo.WorkingDirectory = $ObsDir
        $processStartInfo.UseShellExecute = $false
        $processStartInfo.RedirectStandardOutput = $true
        $processStartInfo.RedirectStandardError = $true
        $processStartInfo.CreateNoWindow = $true

        $process = New-Object System.Diagnostics.Process
        $process.StartInfo = $processStartInfo
        $process.Start() | Out-Null

        $process.Id | Out-File -FilePath $PidFile -Encoding utf8

        Start-Sleep -Seconds 1

        if ($process.HasExited) {
            Write-Host "Error: Failed to start HTTP server" -ForegroundColor Red
            Remove-Item $PidFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
            exit 1
        }

        Write-Host "Simple HTTP server started (PID: $($process.Id))" -ForegroundColor Green
        Write-Host "Access at: http://localhost:$Port" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "Note: This is a basic file server. Create a proper dashboard for full monitoring." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    } else {
        Write-Host "Error: Python not found" -ForegroundColor Red
        exit 1
    }

    # Open browser
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
    Start-Process "http://localhost:$Port"
    exit 0
}

# Start the actual dashboard
Write-Host "Using dashboard: $dashboardScript" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Using interpreter: $($command.Source)" -ForegroundColor Cyan

$env:PORT = $Port
$processStartInfo = New-Object System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo
$processStartInfo.FileName = $command.Source
$processStartInfo.Arguments = "`"$dashboardScript`""
$processStartInfo.WorkingDirectory = $ObsDir
$processStartInfo.UseShellExecute = $false
$processStartInfo.RedirectStandardOutput = $true
$processStartInfo.RedirectStandardError = $true
$processStartInfo.CreateNoWindow = $true

$process = New-Object System.Diagnostics.Process
$process.StartInfo = $processStartInfo
$process.Start() | Out-Null

$process.Id | Out-File -FilePath $PidFile -Encoding utf8

Start-Sleep -Seconds 2

if ($process.HasExited) {
    Write-Host "Error: Dashboard failed to start" -ForegroundColor Red
    Remove-Item $PidFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    exit 1
}

Write-Host "Dashboard started (PID: $($process.Id))" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Access at: http://localhost:$Port" -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Wait a moment then open browser
Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
Start-Process "http://localhost:$Port"

1→#!/usr/bin/env bun
     2→/**
     3→ * RebuildSkill.ts - Auto-rebuild CORE SKILL.md from Components
     4→ *
     5→ * PURPOSE:
     6→ * Automatically rebuilds SKILL.md from Components/ after every response.
     7→ * Ensures SKILL.md stays current if components were edited during the session.
     8→ *
     9→ * TRIGGER: Stop event (via StopOrchestrator)
    10→ *
    11→ * BEHAVIOR:
    12→ * - Checks if any Component file is newer than SKILL.md
    13→ * - If yes, runs BuildSkill.ts to regenerate SKILL.md
    14→ * - Logs rebuild status to stderr
    15→ *
    16→ * DESIGN RATIONALE:
    17→ * - Rebuild on session END (not START) keeps LoadContext fast
    18→ * - Components are source of truth, SKILL.md is generated artifact
    19→ * - No manual `bun BuildSkill.ts` needed - happens automatically
    20→ */
    21→
    22→import { statSync, readdirSync } from 'fs';
    23→import { join } from 'path';
    24→import { spawnSync } from 'child_process';
    25→import { PLATFORM } from '../lib/platform-paths';
    26→
    27→export async function handleRebuildSkill(): Promise<void> {
    28→  const HOME = process.env.USERPROFILE || process.env.HOME;
    29→  const CORE_DIR = join(HOME!, '.claude/skills/PAI');
    30→  const COMPONENTS_DIR = join(CORE_DIR, 'Components');

1→/**
     2→ * recovery-types.ts - Type definitions for the recovery journal system
     3→ */
     4→
     5→export interface RecoveryPoint {
     6→  id: string;                    // UUID for this recovery point
     7→  timestamp: string;             // ISO timestamp
     8→  timestamp_pst: string;         // PST for display
     9→  session_id: string;            // Claude session that triggered
    10→  command: string;               // Original command (truncated)
    11→  command_type: DestructiveType; // Classification
    12→  affected_paths: string[];      // Files/dirs that will be modified
    13→  snapshot_path?: string;        // Path to snapshot archive
    14→  status: 'pending' | 'created' | 'restored' | 'expired';
    15→  file_count?: number;           // Number of files in snapshot
    16→  snapshot_size?: number;        // Size in bytes
    17→}
    18→
    19→export type DestructiveType =
    20→  | 'file_delete'      // rm, unlink
    21→  | 'file_move'        // mv (source files disappear)
    22→  | 'git_reset'        // git reset --hard, git checkout --
    23→  | 'git_clean'        // git clean -fd
    24→  | 'directory_delete' // rm -r, rmdir
    25→  | 'overwrite'        // > redirect, cp that overwrites
    26→  | 'bulk_modify';     // sed -i, find -exec
    27→
    28→export interface DestructivePattern {
    29→  pattern: RegExp;
    30→  type: DestructiveType;

input.content:
# PAI v2.5 - Windows Installation Guide

## Prerequisites

1. **Windows 10/11** (Windows 7/8 not supported)
2. **PowerShell 5.1 or later** (included with Windows 10/11)
3. **PowerShell 7+** (recommended for better performance)
4. **Node.js 18+** or **Bun runtime**
5. **Git** (optional, for version control)
6. **Python 3.8+** (required for VoiceServer)

## Quick Install

### Option 1: Using Bun (Recommended)

```powershell
# 1. Install Bun (if not already installed)
powershell -c "irm bun.sh/install.ps1 | iex"

# 2. Navigate to PAI directory
cd C:\Users\YOUR_NAME\Downloads\Orion\Personal_AI_Infrastructure-main\Releases\v2.5

# 3. Run installation wizard
cd .claude
bun run INSTALL.ts
```

### Option 2: Using Node.js

```powershell
# 1. Install Node.js from https://nodejs.org/

# 2. Navigate to PAI directory
cd C:\Users\YOUR_NAME\Downloads\Orion\Personal_AI_Infrastructure-main\Releases\v2.5

# 3. Run installation wizard
cd .claude
node INSTALL.ts
```

## Installation Wizard

The wizard will prompt for:

1. **Your name**: Used for personalization
2. **Projects directory**: Default is `C:\Users\YOUR_NAME\Projects`
3. **AI name**: What to call your assistant (default: PAI)
4. **ElevenLabs API key**: Optional, for voice notifications

## Post-Installation

### 1. Restart PowerShell

Close and reopen PowerShell to load new environment variables and functions.

### 2. Verify Installation

```powershell
# Check PAI directory
Test-Path $env:USERPROFILE\.claude

# View settings
Get-Content $env:USERPROFILE\.claude\settings.json

# Check PAI version
pai --version
```

### 3. Install Python Dependencies (for VoiceServer)

```powershell
# Install required packages
python -m pip install fastapi uvicorn torch torchaudio
```

### 4. Start VoiceServer (Optional)

```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\.claude\VoiceServer
.\start.ps1

# Test voice
curl.exe -X POST http://localhost:8888/notify -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{\"message\": \"Hello from PAI\"}'

# Stop server
.\stop.ps1
```

### 5. Start Observability Dashboard (Optional)

```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\.claude\Observability
.\start.ps1
```

## Optional: Install as Windows Service

Run VoiceServer as a background service using NSSM:

```powershell
cd $env:USERPROFILE\.claude\VoiceServer
.\install-service.ps1
```

This will:
- Install VoiceServer as a Windows service named "PAIVoiceServer"
- Configure auto-start on boot
- Set up logging
- Start the service immediately

### Service Management

```powershell
# Check service status
Get-Service PAIVoiceServer

# Start service
Start-Service PAIVoiceServer

# Stop service
Stop-Service PAIVoiceServer

# Restart service
Restart-Service PAIVoiceServer

# View logs
Get-Content $env:USERPROFILE\.claude\VoiceServer\logs\service-out.log

# Remove service
nssm remove PAIVoiceServer confirm
```

## Platform-Specific Features

### Windows Terminal vs PowerShell

**Recommended**: Use Windows Terminal for the best experience
- Download from Microsoft Store
- Supports multiple tabs, Unicode, and true color
- Better performance than legacy Console

**PowerShell 7**: Install for cross-platform compatibility
```powershell
winget install Microsoft.PowerShell
```

### Path Handling

PAI on Windows handles paths automatically:
- Backslashes (`\`) work everywhere
- Forward slashes (`/`) also work (Node.js normalizes them)
- Environment variables like `$env:USERPROFILE` expand correctly

### File Permissions

Windows uses ACLs instead of Unix permissions:
- INSTALL.ts uses `icacls` for setting permissions
- Default permissions usually work fine
- Admin privileges required for some operations

## Troubleshooting

### Execution Policy Error

**Problem**: "cannot run scripts because execution policy is restricted"

**Solution**:
```powershell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser
```

### Path Too Long Errors

**Problem**: "The specified path, file name, or both are too long"

Windows has a 260 character path limit (MAX_PATH).

**Solution 1**: Enable long paths (Windows 10+)
```powershell
# Run as Administrator
New-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem" `
    -Name "LongPathsEnabled" -Value 1 -PropertyType DWORD -Force
```

**Solution 2**: Move installation closer to root
```powershell
# Instead of:
C:\Users\LongName\Downloads\Orion\Personal_AI_Infrastructure-main\Releases\v2.5

# Use:
C:\PAI
```

### VoiceServer Won't Start

**Problem**: Port 8888 already in use

**Solution**:
```powershell
# Find process using port
netstat -ano | findstr :8888

# Kill process (replace PID)
taskkill /F /PID <PID>
```

**Problem**: Python not found

**Solution**:
```powershell
# Install Python
winget install Python.Python.3.11

# Or download from: https://www.python.org/downloads/
```

**Problem**: Missing dependencies

**Solution**:
```powershell
# Install required packages
python -m pip install fastapi uvicorn torch torchaudio soundfile
```

### Git Commands Not Found

**Problem**: git command not recognized

**Solution**:
```powershell
winget install Git.Git
```

Then restart PowerShell.

### PowerShell Profile Issues

**Problem**: PAI alias not working after installation

**Solution**:
```powershell
# Check if profile was created
Test-Path $env:USERPROFILE\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1

# View profile
Get-Content $env:USERPROFILE\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1

# Manually add alias (add to profile)
function pai { bun $env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills\PAI\Tools\pai.ts $args }
```

### NSSM Service Installation Fails

**Problem**: NSSM not found or installation fails

**Solution**:
```powershell
# Install NSSM manually
# 1. Download from: https://nssm.cc/download
# 2. Extract to C:\nssm
# 3. Add to PATH: $env:Path += ";C:\nssm"

# Or use winget (Windows 11)
winget install nssm
```

## Uninstall

```powershell
# Stop services
Stop-Service PAIVoiceServer

# Remove service
nssm remove PAIVoiceServer confirm

# Remove PAI directory
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force $env:USERPROFILE\.claude

# Remove PowerShell profile config
# Edit: $env:USERPROFILE\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1
# Remove PAI-related lines (look for "# PAI alias" marker)
```

## Windows-Specific Configuration

### Settings.json

The installation wizard creates a Windows-specific settings.json:

```json
{
  "platform": "win32",
  "techStack": {
    "terminal": "windows-terminal",
    "packageManager": "bun"
  },
  "statusLine": {
    "command": "${PAI_DIR}/statusline-command.ps1"
  },
  "paths": {
    "home": "C:\\Users\\YOUR_NAME",
    "claude": "C:\\Users\\YOUR_NAME\\.claude",
    "projects": "C:\\Users\\YOUR_NAME\\Projects"
  }
}
```

### Environment Variables

PAI uses these environment variables on Windows:
- `$env:USERPROFILE` - User home directory
- `$env:PAI_DIR` - PAI installation directory (set by installer)
- `$env:PROJECTS_DIR` - Projects root directory (optional)

### File Locations

Windows-specific file locations:
- **Config**: `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\settings.json`
- **Logs**: `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\VoiceServer\logs\`
- **Memory**: `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\MEMORY\`
- **Hooks**: `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\hooks\`
- **Skills**: `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\`

## Performance Tips

### Use PowerShell 7

PowerShell 7 is faster and more compatible than Windows PowerShell 5.1:

```powershell
# Install PowerShell 7
winget install Microsoft.PowerShell

# Run PAI in PowerShell 7
pwsh
pai
```

### Enable Windows Terminal

Windows Terminal provides better performance and features:
- Tabbed interface
- GPU-accelerated text rendering
- True color support
- Unicode support

### Exclude from Antivirus Scans

Add PAI directories to Windows Defender exclusions for better performance:

```powershell
# Run as Administrator
Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
```

## Next Steps

1. Read the main README.md
2. Explore skills in `.claude\skills\`
3. Configure settings in `.claude\settings.json`
4. Start using PAI with Claude Code

## Getting Help

- **GitHub Issues**: https://github.com/danielmiessler/PAI/issues
- **Documentation**: `.claude\README.md`
- **Troubleshooting**: Check logs in `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\VoiceServer\logs\`

## Compatibility Notes

### What Works on Windows

✅ Full installation wizard
✅ VoiceServer with Windows audio
✅ PowerShell status line
✅ Observability dashboard
✅ All PAI skills
✅ Hooks and automation
✅ Git integration
✅ Claude Code integration

### Limitations

⚠️ Some Unix-specific skills may not work
⚠️ Terminal apps may have limited functionality
⚠️ File permissions work differently

### Workarounds

For Unix-specific features:
- Use WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)
- Use Git Bash for Unix commands
- Use WSL2 for full Linux compatibility

```powershell
# Install WSL
wsl --install

# Use PAI from WSL
wsl
cd ~/.claude
```

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Cross-Platform Path Utilities
     3→ *
     4→ * Provides platform-aware path handling for Windows, macOS, and Linux.
     5→ * All hooks should import from this file instead of using process.env.HOME directly.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import * as path from 'path';
     9→import * as os from 'os';
    10→
    11→export const PLATFORM = process.platform;
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * Get the home directory in a cross-platform way.
    15→ * Windows: USERPROFILE environment variable
    16→ * macOS/Linux: HOME environment variable
    17→ */
    18→export const HOME_DIR = PLATFORM === 'win32'
    19→  ? process.env.USERPROFILE || os.homedir()
    20→  : process.env.HOME || os.homedir();
    21→
    22→/**
    23→ * Get the PAI directory (root of the Claude/PAI system)
    24→ * Uses PAI_DIR env var if set, otherwise defaults to ~/.claude
    25→ */
    26→export const PAI_DIR = process.env.PAI_DIR || path.join(HOME_DIR, '.claude');
    27→
    28→/**
    29→ * Get the Claude directory (alias for PAI_DIR for compatibility)
    30→ */
    31→export const CLAUDE_DIR = PAI_DIR;
    32→
    33→/**
    34→ * Platform-specific path separator
    35→ */
    36→export const PATH_SEPARATOR = path.sep;
    37→
    38→/**
    39→ * Join path segments using the platform-appropriate separator
    40→ */
    41→export function joinPath(...segments: string[]): string {
    42→  return path.join(...segments);
    43→}
    44→
    45→/**
    46→ * Normalize a path to use the platform-appropriate separator
    47→ * Converts forward slashes to backslashes on Windows
    48→ */
    49→export function normalizePath(filePath: string): string {
    50→  return path.normalize(filePath.replace(/\//g, path.sep));
    51→}
    52→
    53→/**
    54→ * Get the temp directory in a cross-platform way
    55→ */
    56→export const TEMP_DIR = PLATFORM === 'win32'
    57→  ? process.env.TEMP || process.env.TMP || os.tmpdir()
    58→  : os.tmpdir();
    59→
    60→/**
    61→ * Get the session start file path for timing
    62→ * Windows: %TEMP%\pai-session-start.txt
    63→ * macOS/Linux: /tmp/pai-session-start.txt
    64→ */
    65→export const SESSION_START_FILE = PLATFORM === 'win32'
    66→  ? path.join(TEMP_DIR, 'pai-session-start.txt')
    67→  : '/tmp/pai-session-start.txt';
    68→

1→/**
     2→ * recovery-types.ts - Type definitions for the recovery journal system
     3→ */
     4→
     5→import { getPaiDir } from './paths';
     6→
     7→export interface RecoveryPoint {
     8→  id: string;                    // UUID for this recovery point
     9→  timestamp: string;             // ISO timestamp
    10→  timestamp_pst: string;         // PST for display
    11→  session_id: string;            // Claude session that triggered
    12→  command: string;               // Original command (truncated)
    13→  command_type: DestructiveType; // Classification
    14→  affected_paths: string[];      // Files/dirs that will be modified
    15→  snapshot_path?: string;        // Path to snapshot archive
    16→  status: 'pending' | 'created' | 'restored' | 'expired';
    17→  file_count?: number;           // Number of files in snapshot
    18→  snapshot_size?: number;        // Size in bytes
    19→}
